regex-string.comment: A string literal immediately after `=~` (a String refinement or `where` predicate, e.g. `value =~ "[a-z]+"`) is a REGEX, not plain text — scope it string.regexp and highlight its structure so escapes/classes/quantifiers/groups read distinctly. Placed BEFORE #strings and #operators in the patterns list so it wins the `=~ "` sequence: at the `=` both this and #operators/#strings could start, and rule order decides. The supported regex grammar (see compiler/vela-frontend/src/regex.rs) is alternation `|`, grouping `()`, classes `[…]`, escapes `\d \w \s \D \W \S \<c>`, `.`, and quantifiers `* + ? {m} {m,} {m,n}`.
regex-string.name: string.regexp.vela

patterns.comment: Escapes and shorthand classes: \d \w \s (and negated) plus any escaped metacharacter (\. \[ \\ …). Listed first so `\[` does not open a character class and `\.` is not read as the dot atom.
patterns.name: constant.character.escape.vela

template-tag.comment: The tag of a tagged template (`sql"…"`, RFC-0007 Phase 2): a lowerCamelCase identifier directly against a `"`. Scoped entity.name.function so the tag reads as the call it is. Placed before #strings; the string itself is highlighted by #strings on the next position.
template-tag.name: entity.name.function.vela

capabilities.comment: Capability modifiers on a parameter's type: `fn f(t: consume Token)`, `fn f(c: modify Counter)`, `fn f(cfg: share Config)`, `read`. These are CONTEXTUAL, not reserved — the lexer emits them as plain identifiers and the parser only treats them as capabilities in `: <cap> <Type>` position (parse_capability). So match them contextually — between the `:` and a PascalCase type — rather than as keywords, which keeps `let share = 1` a normal binding. The `:` is captured (and re-scoped as separator) instead of a variable-length lookbehind, which oniguruma does not reliably support. Scoped storage.modifier so themes render it distinctly from both keywords and variables.

patterns.comment: Interpolated string "a\{expr}b" (RFC-0007). Holes use \{ ... } so plain { } stay literal.
patterns.name: string.quoted.double.vela

string-escapes.comment: String escapes. \{ is NOT here: it opens an interpolation hole (below), and { is already a literal char in Vela strings, so there is no `\{` escape.
string-escapes.name: constant.character.escape.vela

interpolation.comment: A \{ ... } interpolation hole. The closing } is consumed by `end` and colored via endCaptures. Inner patterns deliberately OMIT } — an included } here would match before `end` and leave the hole unterminated. Nested braces (e.g. a block expr) are a known limitation: the hole closes at the first } not inside a nested string. NOTE: the delimiter scopes intentionally reuse the `punctuation.definition.template-expression.*` family rather than a generic `punctuation.definition.interpolation` — Dark+ (and most themes) theme the former but not the latter, so this is what makes \{ and } actually render in a distinct color instead of falling back to the surrounding string color.
interpolation.name: meta.interpolation.vela

test-keyword.comment: `test` (RFC-0015) is a CONTEXTUAL starter, not a reserved word: the parser treats it as a test declaration only when a string literal follows (`test "name" { .. }`), so `let test = 1` stays an ordinary binding. Match it as a keyword only directly before a `"`, mirroring how the parser recognizes it. Placed before #keywords/#function-calls/#identifiers so it wins that position.
test-keyword.name: keyword.control.vela

function-calls.comment: A lowerCamelCase identifier directly before `(` is a function/method call (or the name in `fn name(...)`) — entity.name.function → yellow in Dark+, distinct from plain variables. PascalCase constructors (Circle(1), Some(x)) are NOT matched here: #types is included earlier and wins, keeping variants teal. Keywords are also included earlier, so `if (...)` stays a keyword.
function-calls.name: entity.name.function.vela

types.comment: Type and enum-variant names. Vela's style is PascalCase for types/variants (Shape, Circle, Option, Int64, Some, Ok) and lowerCamelCase for variables/functions, so a PascalCase identifier is reliably a type or variant. This subsumes the built-ins (all PascalCase) and colors user-defined types/variants the same as built-ins, instead of maintaining an explicit list. Scoped as support.type → teal in Dark+.
types.name: support.type.vela

operators.comment: Multi-char operators listed first so >= is not split into > then =. Order in the alternation matters: oniguruma picks the first alternative that matches at the position.
operators.name: keyword.operator.vela

punctuation.comment: Brackets and delimiters in normal (top-level) code. Includes } (block close).

hole-punctuation.comment: Same as #punctuation but WITHOUT } — the interpolation hole's `end` owns the closing brace, and an included } would shadow it and never close the hole.
